description:
You are a .NET developer at a clinical research organization. The ComplianceReporter solution (open in Visual Studio 2022) implements an adverse event reporting engine for clinical trials. It has three projects: Engine (business logic library), Tests (xUnit test suite), and App (console application).

The solution compiles, but 7 of 12 unit tests fail. Debug and fix all issues in the Engine project so that all 12 tests pass. Do NOT modify the test project — all tests are correct. Some methods may need implementation — see their XML doc comments for specifications. After all tests pass, build the solution in Release mode with 0 errors, then run the App project to process adverse_events.csv and generate a compliance report at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\compliance_report.txt. Write a debug report to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\debug_report.txt listing each bug found and how you fixed it.

Windows user: Docker, password: GymAnything123!

bugs: Severity classification labels swapped: Grade 3 returns Moderate instead of Severe, Grade 2 returns Severe instead of Moderate
Onset days calculation has reversed date subtraction: enrollmentDate - eventDate instead of eventDate - enrollmentDate
Event rate uses integer division: eventCount / totalPatients truncates to 0 for fractional results
stubs: CalculateWilsonScoreInterval: Wilson score confidence interval for binomial proportions
DetectExpeditedReportingFlags: FDA 15-day expedited reporting filter (Grade>=4, Death/Life-Threatening, OnsetDays<=15)
